fields.description:
## Why
The auto-lander is a long-running service; it must deploy like the review-bot and log to its OWN service logs ‚Äî writing to `session_log` would blur that type's purpose (it is for agentic sessions, not CI/infra processes).

## What
Containerize the service and wire it into docker-compose (single instance) on the existing Gerrit box; route logs to container stdout ‚Üí the existing CloudWatch monitoring.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S2** (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`, 0ee8-08de-bf0f-45ec) ‚Äî the core loop: this story packages and deploys the loop service, so there must be a service to containerize; the deploy is otherwise independent of the failure/command stories.

## Scope
Deploy + observability + single-instance guard. Not the loop logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` + a compose service in `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`.
- [ ] **Single-instance guard:** the container acquires a POSIX advisory **file lock** (`flock` on a lockfile in its state volume) on start; a second copy fails to acquire it and exits. This `flock` is the **sole** single-instance mechanism ‚Äî do NOT rely on `deploy.replicas: 1` (a Swarm-only key silently ignored by plain `docker compose`).
- [ ] **Credentials (AC2 mechanism):** `autolander-bot`/RebarBotNava's `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` is a **server-level capability** granted in `All-Projects` `refs/meta/config` (`[capability]` / service-user grant), not a per-project label ACL; plus submit permission on `refs/heads/*`. The Gerrit HTTP token is provisioned as an SSM **SecureString** at the concrete path `/rebar/autolander/gerrit-http-token` (added in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, fetched by `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`) ‚Äî never inlined.
- [ ] **Observability (AC3):** logs go to container stdout; `infra/scripts/observability.sh` greps the container (`CONTAINER_NAME=compose-autolander-1`) for an `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` marker and emits `PutMetricData` to `rebar/host:autolander_errors`, with an alarm in `infra/terraform/monitoring_*.tf`. The service NEVER writes `session_log`.
- [ ] **Liveness/restart (AC4):** a `HEALTHCHECK` in `Dockerfile.autolander` + `restart: unless-stopped` in compose; a hung/crashed container is caught by the health check + restart policy (surfaced via the `autolander_errors` metric). (The `wipChain`-timeout parking of a stuck stack is loop logic owned by S2/S3, not this deploy story.)
